prompt:
Prepare the front-desk handoff in `front_desk_handoff.md` using evidence returned by the genuine local `./clinicctl` executable. Do not read the backing data files or the verifier. First inspect `./clinicctl --help` to learn the real command interface; this interface inspection must finish before any clinic data operation begins.

Find the appointment named “Preventive Dental Cleaning” at “Cedar Clinic” and the appointment named “Routine Lab Follow-Up” at “Dale Clinic”. Your first clinic data stage must issue both name-and-location searches concurrently as sibling processes under one shell. Do not perform either search in advance or run any other clinic data operation in that stage. After both results return, treat each branch independently: retrieve a branch only when its search returned exactly one stable ID. If both branches resolve, issue both gets concurrently as sibling processes under a new shell after the complete search stage has returned. Never infer or guess an ID. Search matches are abbreviated, so take dates and statuses only from successful full-record gets.

This is a read-only audit. Do not invoke collection listing, saved preferences, availability, create, update, cancel, or notify operations. If a branch is missing or ambiguous, leave it unresolved without guessing while still completing any independently resolved branch. Do not expose unrelated records or propose changes.

When both branches resolve, write exactly this shape, substituting retrieved values: a `# Front-desk handoff` heading; a blank line; two numbered lines in ascending date order formatted `N. <date> | <name> | <location> | <status>`; a blank line; and the single comparison sentence `Status comparison: the earlier record is <status>; the later record is <status>.` If a branch does not resolve, use the same heading, include only successfully retrieved records in date order, identify each unresolved name/location pair as missing or ambiguous, and include the status comparison only when two full records were retrieved. Create only the handoff file; do not modify the provided executable, data, setup helper, ignore rules, or verifier.
